Across Aotearoa New Zealand, AI is furthest along in agriculture and healthcare, with the public sector also scaling, but mainly in supervised operational work rather than autonomous high-stakes decisions. Concrete use is clearest in emergency-department clinical documentation, livestock and pasture monitoring, fraud and anomaly detection, and classroom planning support, while trust, data quality, skills, privacy, interoperability, and proof of value still limit broader frontline adoption.

This living whitepaper currently tracks AI in academic research, agriculture, creative industries, education, finance, healthcare, and the public sector in Aotearoa New Zealand.

Updated 11 March 2026
